L1.area: JetStream data persistence and stream management

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where entries in a compressed tar archive used during data restoration are extracted without validating that the resulting file paths remain within the intended directory. An attacker who can supply a crafted archive can write files to arbitrary locations on the filesystem as the process user.

L3.area: JetStream stream snapshot restoration and archive extraction
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where entries in a compressed tar archive used during JetStream stream snapshot restoration are extracted without validating that the resulting file paths remain within the intended staging directory. An attacker with access to the privileged system account who can supply a crafted snapshot archive can write files to arbitrary locations on the filesystem.

ground_truth.reason: The RestoreStream function extracts tar archive entries using path.Join without verifying that the resulting file path stays within the intended snapshot directory, allowing directory traversal via crafted archive entries to write arbitrary files.
